trial_details.trajectory:
The pod is in the ErrImageNeverPull status, which means the image specified in the pod's configuration cannot be pulled.The issue is that the pod is trying to pull an image named "misspeling-app:latset" which does not exist. The pod is in the ErrImageNeverPull state.The patch command updates the image name in the deployment spec from "misspeling-app:latset" to "misspelling-app:latest".

FIX COMPLETE

The diagnosis was that the pod was failing to pull the image "misspeling-app:latset" due to a typo in the image name. I fixed this by patching the deployment to use the correct image name "misspelling-app:latest".

## COMMANDS EXECUTED:
1. $ kubectl get pod misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-hbpm5 -n case6-test
   Output: NAME                                      READY   STATUS              RESTARTS   AGE
misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-hbpm5   0/1     ErrImageNeverPull   0          12s...
2. $ kubectl describe pod misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-hbpm5 -n case6-test
   Output: Name:             misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-hbpm5
Namespace:        case6-test
Priority:         0
Service Account:  default
Node:             minikube/192.168.49.2
Start Time:       Thu, 25 D...
3. $ kubectl patch deployment misspelling-deployment -n case6-test --type=json -p='[{"op":"replace","path":"/spec/template/spec/containers/0/image","value":"misspelling-app:latest"}]'
   Output: deployment.apps/misspelling-deployment patched...
